verses.text: Solomon declares the LORD has chosen to dwell in 'thick darkness,' yet now has a settled house; then he turns and blesses the whole congregation. The God of heaven stoops to dwell with His people. He hides His full glory in mystery, yet draws near to bless those gathered before Him.

Solomon declares the LORD has chosen to dwell in 'thick darkness,' yet now has a settled house; then he turns and blesses the whole congregation. The God of heaven stoops to dwell with His people. He hides His full glory in mystery, yet draws near to bless those gathered before Him.

Solomon declares the LORD has chosen to dwell in 'thick darkness,' yet now has a settled house; then he turns and blesses the whole congregation. The God of heaven stoops to dwell with His people. He hides His full glory in mystery, yet draws near to bless those gathered before Him.

Solomon blesses the LORD, recalling how God chose Jerusalem for His name and David to rule, fulfilling by His hand what He spoke with His mouth. Praise rehearses God's faithfulness. What He promises He performs, and remembering His past mercies is the truest worship.

Solomon blesses the LORD, recalling how God chose Jerusalem for His name and David to rule, fulfilling by His hand what He spoke with His mouth. Praise rehearses God's faithfulness. What He promises He performs, and remembering His past mercies is the truest worship.

Solomon blesses the LORD, recalling how God chose Jerusalem for His name and David to rule, fulfilling by His hand what He spoke with His mouth. Praise rehearses God's faithfulness. What He promises He performs, and remembering His past mercies is the truest worship.

Solomon tells how building the temple was in David's heart, but God reserved the work for the son; now it stands, the ark within. The father's holy desire bears fruit in the next generation. God honours the longing even when He assigns the labour to another.

Solomon tells how building the temple was in David's heart, but God reserved the work for the son; now it stands, the ark within. The father's holy desire bears fruit in the next generation. God honours the longing even when He assigns the labour to another.

Solomon tells how building the temple was in David's heart, but God reserved the work for the son; now it stands, the ark within. The father's holy desire bears fruit in the next generation. God honours the longing even when He assigns the labour to another.

Solomon kneels on the bronze scaffold before all Israel and spreads out his hands to heaven. The great king bows low in prayer. The higher our place, the humbler our posture should be before God; true majesty kneels before the King of kings.

Solomon kneels on the bronze scaffold before all Israel and spreads out his hands to heaven. The great king bows low in prayer. The higher our place, the humbler our posture should be before God; true majesty kneels before the King of kings.

Solomon prays: there is no God like the LORD, who keeps covenant and mercy with His servants — now let the promise to David's house stand sure. He pleads God's faithfulness back to Him. The surest ground of prayer is the word God has already spoken.

Solomon prays: there is no God like the LORD, who keeps covenant and mercy with His servants — now let the promise to David's house stand sure. He pleads God's faithfulness back to Him. The surest ground of prayer is the word God has already spoken.

Solomon prays: there is no God like the LORD, who keeps covenant and mercy with His servants — now let the promise to David's house stand sure. He pleads God's faithfulness back to Him. The surest ground of prayer is the word God has already spoken.

Solomon prays: there is no God like the LORD, who keeps covenant and mercy with His servants — now let the promise to David's house stand sure. He pleads God's faithfulness back to Him. The surest ground of prayer is the word God has already spoken.

Solomon marvels: will God indeed dwell with men, when heaven itself cannot contain Him? Yet he begs that God's eyes be open toward this house, to hear prayer made here. The infinite God cannot be housed, but He stoops to meet His people where they seek Him.

Solomon marvels: will God indeed dwell with men, when heaven itself cannot contain Him? Yet he begs that God's eyes be open toward this house, to hear prayer made here. The infinite God cannot be housed, but He stoops to meet His people where they seek Him.

Solomon marvels: will God indeed dwell with men, when heaven itself cannot contain Him? Yet he begs that God's eyes be open toward this house, to hear prayer made here. The infinite God cannot be housed, but He stoops to meet His people where they seek Him.

Solomon marvels: will God indeed dwell with men, when heaven itself cannot contain Him? Yet he begs that God's eyes be open toward this house, to hear prayer made here. The infinite God cannot be housed, but He stoops to meet His people where they seek Him.

Solomon asks that when men swear before the altar, God would judge between them, condemning the wicked and justifying the righteous. He commits human disputes to heaven's bar. God is the final Judge, who alone sees the heart and renders to each his due.

Solomon asks that when men swear before the altar, God would judge between them, condemning the wicked and justifying the righteous. He commits human disputes to heaven's bar. God is the final Judge, who alone sees the heart and renders to each his due.

When Israel is defeated for sin, yet turns and confesses and prays toward this house, Solomon asks God to forgive and restore them. Defeat is meant to drive us back to God. He waits to receive the penitent and bring them home again.

When Israel is defeated for sin, yet turns and confesses and prays toward this house, Solomon asks God to forgive and restore them. Defeat is meant to drive us back to God. He waits to receive the penitent and bring them home again.

When the heavens are shut for sin and no rain falls, if the people turn and pray, Solomon asks God to forgive and send rain. The withheld blessing is a call to repent. God sends drought to teach, and mercy the moment His people turn.

When the heavens are shut for sin and no rain falls, if the people turn and pray, Solomon asks God to forgive and send rain. The withheld blessing is a call to repent. God sends drought to teach, and mercy the moment His people turn.

In famine or plague, when each man knows the plague of his own heart and spreads his hands toward this house, Solomon asks God to hear and render to each his ways, that they may fear Him. Affliction searches the conscience. God answers the prayer that owns its own sin.

In famine or plague, when each man knows the plague of his own heart and spreads his hands toward this house, Solomon asks God to hear and render to each his ways, that they may fear Him. Affliction searches the conscience. God answers the prayer that owns its own sin.

In famine or plague, when each man knows the plague of his own heart and spreads his hands toward this house, Solomon asks God to hear and render to each his ways, that they may fear Him. Affliction searches the conscience. God answers the prayer that owns its own sin.

In famine or plague, when each man knows the plague of his own heart and spreads his hands toward this house, Solomon asks God to hear and render to each his ways, that they may fear Him. Affliction searches the conscience. God answers the prayer that owns its own sin.

Solomon prays even for the stranger from a far country who comes for God's name's sake — that God would hear him too, so all peoples may know and fear the LORD. God's house was never meant for one nation only. His mercy reaches the outsider who seeks Him.

Solomon prays even for the stranger from a far country who comes for God's name's sake — that God would hear him too, so all peoples may know and fear the LORD. God's house was never meant for one nation only. His mercy reaches the outsider who seeks Him.

When God's people go out to battle at His bidding and pray toward this city and house, Solomon asks God to maintain their cause. War is no excuse to forget prayer. Those who fight in God's will may carry their need straight to His throne.

When God's people go out to battle at His bidding and pray toward this city and house, Solomon asks God to maintain their cause. War is no excuse to forget prayer. Those who fight in God's will may carry their need straight to His throne.

Foreseeing captivity for sin, Solomon asks that if the exiles repent in a far land and pray toward their home, God would hear from heaven and forgive, 'for there is no man which sinneth not.' No distance puts the penitent beyond mercy; a turning heart is always heard.

Foreseeing captivity for sin, Solomon asks that if the exiles repent in a far land and pray toward their home, God would hear from heaven and forgive, 'for there is no man which sinneth not.' No distance puts the penitent beyond mercy; a turning heart is always heard.

Foreseeing captivity for sin, Solomon asks that if the exiles repent in a far land and pray toward their home, God would hear from heaven and forgive, 'for there is no man which sinneth not.' No distance puts the penitent beyond mercy; a turning heart is always heard.

Foreseeing captivity for sin, Solomon asks that if the exiles repent in a far land and pray toward their home, God would hear from heaven and forgive, 'for there is no man which sinneth not.' No distance puts the penitent beyond mercy; a turning heart is always heard.

Solomon closes: let God's eyes be open to this place, arise into His resting place, and 'remember the mercies of David.' He rests the whole plea on God's covenant love. Our final hope in prayer is not our worth but the steadfast mercies God has pledged.
